title: Cremanium croceum
taxonomy: Cremanium croceum is classified within the family Scleromitrinae, which is a subfamily of the genus Amanita, and it belongs to the larger phylum Basidiomycota. This classification places it within the kingdom Fungi. The Scleromitrinae subfamily is characterized by its unique mushroom species that are often found in tropical regions. Further classification within the kingdom Fungi places Cremanium croceum as a type of basidiomycete, a group of fungi that produces spores by meiosis. The specific classification of Cremanium croceum allows for a more detailed understanding of its evolutionary relationships and characteristics.
